question: In the temptation narratives, how does Jesus relive Israel’s wilderness story (Deuteronomy quotes), and what apologetic point does that make about ‘fulfillment’ rather than ‘random proof-texting’?

choices.A: Jesus reenacts Israel’s testing and answers with Deuteronomy, showing fulfillment as a coherent narrative pattern, not isolated verse-snatching.
choices.B: Jesus quotes Deuteronomy only because it was popular, with no deeper link.
choices.C: It shows Jesus rejected the Torah.
choices.D: It proves the OT has no relevance to the NT.

teaching.title: Israel’s story replayed—and repaired
teaching.body: Matthew and Luke show Jesus in the wilderness answering temptation with Deuteronomy—texts associated with Israel’s wilderness testing. That structure reads like deliberate typology: Jesus embodies Israel’s vocation and succeeds where Israel failed. Apologetically, it supports a ‘fulfillment-as-pattern’ reading rather than cherry-picked quotations.
